data.title: Blink Charging regains compliance with Nasdaq periodic filing requirement
data.description:
• Blink Charging (NASDAQ:BLNK) received a letter from The Nasdaq Stock Market on April 10, 2025 informing that the company has regained compliance with Nasdaq’s periodic filing requirement.
  • On April 9, 2025, Blink filed its Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024.
  • BLNK +6.56% premarket to $0.7383.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BNY Mellon Q1 profit slides Q/Q as fee revenue, NII, deposits weigh
data.description:
Bank of New York Mellon (NYSE:BK) posted Friday Q1 earnings that topped Wall Street expectations, but fell from the prior three-month period, as fee revenue, net interest income and deposits all softened. Even so, the firm recorded positive operating leverage, which improved pre-tax margins and return on tangible common equity.

Q1 adjusted EPS of $1.58, surpassing the $1.49 average analyst estimate, fell from $1.72 in the prior quarter, but rose from $1.29 a year earlier.

Total revenue of $4.79B, also exceeding the $4.77B expected, slid from $4.85B in Q4 and advanced from $4.53B in Q1 2024.

BK shares dropped 3% moments after the opening bell.

Net interest income dipped to $1.16B from $1.19B in the previous quarter and climbed from $1.04B in the year-ago period.

Fee revenue fell 3% Q/Q and increased 3% Y/Y to $3.40B.

Provision for credit losses retreated to $18M from $20M in Q4 and $27M in Q1 2024.

"Looking ahead, we are prepared for a wide range of macroeconomic and market scenarios as the outlook for the operating environment is becoming more uncertain," President and CEO Robin Vince said in a statement.

Assets under custody/administration totaled $53.1T, up from $52.1T in Q4 and $48.8T a year before.

Average deposits slipped 1% sequentially to $282.5B, while average loans of $69.7B gained 1% over the same period.

Conference call at 11:00 a.m. ET.

data.title: Bruker reports preliminary Q1 revenue above estimates
data.description:
• Bruker Corporation (NASDAQ:BRKR) reported preliminary revenue for Q1 2025, in a range of $795M to $800M, above consensus of $757.03M.
  • Stock is up about 3% premarket.
  • This estimated range implies reported revenue growth of approximately 10% year-over-year, with low-double digit percentage constant exchange rate revenue growth, Bruker said in a statement.

data.title: Fortuna Mining to sell Yaramoko mine, Burkina Faso assets in $130M deal
data.description:
Fortuna Mining (NYSE:FSM) +4.1% pre-market Friday after saying it agreed to sell its interest in Roxgold Sanu, which owns and operates the Yaramoko mine, and the company's three other wholly-owned Burkina Faso subsidiaries to privately-held Soleil Resources International for a combined $130M.

"Considering the limited remaining life of mineral reserves at Yaramoko (approximately one year), the cessation of our exploration activities in-country, and the increasingly challenging business climate in Burkina Faso, the transaction represents a prudent exit that optimizes value [and] avoids approximately US$20M in future mine closure liabilities," President and CEO Jorge Ganoza said.

On Thursday, Fortuna (NYSE:FSM) reported Q1 gold equivalent production of 103,459 oz, down 8% Y/Y, and reiterated FY 2025 production guidance of 334K-373K oz of gold and 900K-1M oz of silver, or 380K-422K GEOs.

data.title: U.S-China trade tensions put BYD and Geely's expansion plans at risk
data.description:
• Escalating U.S.-China trade tensions are frustrating Chinese EV-maker BYD Company’s (OTCPK:BYDDY) (OTCPK:BYDDF) and Geely Automotive’s (GELYF, GELYY) plans to expand manufacturing into Latin America, Reuters reported, citing two sources familiar with the matter.
  • BYD (OTCPK:BYDDY) (OTCPK:BYDDF) had plans to start production at a Mexican plant, while Geely (GELYF, GELYY) reached an agreement with France’s Renault (OTCPK:RNLSY) (OTCPK:RNSDF) to use Renault’s Brazilian production facilities and to take a minority stake in Renault’s (OTCPK:RNLSY) (OTCPK:RNSDF) Latin American business.
  • However, both projects hinge on Beijing’s approval, which could take longer than usual given the geopolitical climate and restrictions on Chinese imports and technology transfers.
  • Reuters’ sources claim that “discussions were still fluid and subject to change.”

data.title: SNDL expands listing to Canadian Securities Exchange
data.description:
• Cannabis operator SNDL (NASDAQ:SNDL) announced Friday that its common shares have begun trading on the Canadian Securities Exchange (CSE) under the symbol "SNDL," with effect from Apr. 11, giving the company an additional option to raise capital.
  • With Q4 results in March, the Calgary-based firm, which has been trading on Nasdaq, disclosed that it has applied for an additional listing on CSE, and the trading is expected to go live sometime in April.
  • The company said that CSE listing is set to give it additional flexibility to raise capital and provide its shareholders with an opportunity to trade in the Canadian market in Canadian dollars.
  • "Listing on the CSE enhances SNDL's structural flexibility and creates optionality for shareholders while reinforcing our commitment to the Canadian market," CEO Zach George added.

data.title: Unity Bancorp GAAP EPS of $1.13 in-line
data.description:
• Unity Bancorp press release (NASDAQ:UNTY): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.13 in-line.
  • James A. Hughes, President and CEO, commented on the financial results: “We are pleased to announce another strong quarter for Unity Bancorp, Inc. We earned $11.6 million in net income, or $1.13 per diluted share, representing 1.83% ROA and 15.56% ROE.

data.title: KLA, Nova and Entegris keep top pick tags at Citi
data.description:
Citi maintained KLA Corp. (NASDAQ:KLAC), Nova (NASDAQ:NVMI) and Entegris (NASDAQ:ENTG) as its top picks while updating its Wafer Fab Equipment, or WFE, sector's outlook.

Analysts led by Atif Malik said they adjusted 2025/2026 estimates and lowered the price targets on Buy-rated Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT) to $170 from $202, KLA to $800 from $910, and Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) to $80 from $87 using consistent valuation methodology.

The analysts added that they see the group stuck in phase 1 of Citi's stock picking framework with attractive risk-reward 12 months to 18 months out. 

Malik and his team said that while semi caps have relatively low 10% to 20% U.S. sales exposure and have been diversifying away from China in recent years, the impact of new tariffs in the rest of Asia impacts them.

Additional impacts may come from tariffs on raw materials like high purity wafers and photoresist, according to the analysts.

The analysts noted that they lowered 2025/26 WFE to -5%/+2% from the prior estimate of +3%/+6% (Street +8%/+6%) to better align with Citi’s global gross domestic product, or GDP, contraction view of -0.7% in 2025 on higher tariffs and global uncertainty.
